entry.title: Run the Readiness Check 2.0 without SAP_APPL component installed
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Is it possible to run the Readiness Check 2.0 in a system, where the SAP_APPL component is not installed?

Best regards,
Customer 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Run the Readiness Check 2.0 without SAP_APPL componen...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

A system conversion to SAP S/4HANA is only possible for a SAP ERP system (e.g. a full stack ERP installation where all ERP components like SAP-APPL, EA-APPL etc. are installed).

For a standard Netweaver, Business Suite Foundation system or others without SAP-APPL, a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA is technically not possible and not supported. Hence also Readiness Check is not applicable to this system.

The transition path to SAP S/4HANA for such a system would be to take a greenfield approach and migrate any required E-Recruiting data into an already existing or newly installed SAP S/4HANA system.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: OS upgrade SAP ECC Suite on HANA
entry.content:
Dear Support,

We are planning a technological renewal of our operating system infrastructure.


Our SAP system is SAP ECC on NW 7.4 SP16 with PERSON_NAME DB version 2.00.059.PHONE_NUMBER
We are planning an upgrade from RHEL 7.x to 9.x - with clustering of ASCS and ERS resources.
We also have two application servers (PAS and AAS) on different server.


Following this guide:

 https://access.redhat.com/articles/3569681#overview to realize ASCS/ERS cluster.

This is the question: based on above guide (paragraph 2.3) PAS and AAS are installed on separated servers besides of ASCS/ERS cluster servers, we ask you to confirm that we can install PAS and AAS on the same servers of ASCS/ERS cluster.Please we kindly request your feedback


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: OS upgrade SAP ECC Suite on HANA
entry.content:
Yes, you can install PAS (Primary Application Server) and AAS (Additional Application Server) on the same servers as ASCS/ERS cluster. However, it's important to note that this configuration may not be optimal for performance and high availability.

The ASCS (ABAP SAP Central Services) and ERS (Enqueue Replication Server) are critical components of the SAP system, and they are usually installed on separate servers to ensure high availability and performance. If you install the PAS and AAS on the same servers, they will compete for resources with the ASCS and ERS, which could potentially impact the performance and availability of your SAP system.

Therefore, while it's technically possible to install PAS and AAS on the same servers as ASCS/ERS cluster, it's generally recommended to install them on separate servers for optimal performance and high availability.

However, your specific requirements and constraints may dictate otherwise. If you have limited hardware resources, or if your SAP system is not heavily used, then installing PAS and AAS on the same servers as ASCS/ERS cluster might be a viable option.

In any case, you should carefully evaluate your specific needs and constraints before making a decision.

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Business Application Studio – June Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

As we cross the halfway mark of the year, it’s the perfect time to reflect on our progress and introduce the exciting new features we've added over the past few months.
Here’s what’s new:

 

Enhancing SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP BTP, ABAP Environment

Following up on Franziska's March update, we're excited to expand the capabilities of SAPUI5 adaptation projects. Now, you can unlock the full potential of SAP applications in SAP BTP, ABAP environment: We've enhanced the SAPUI5 flexibility to allow developers to customize standard SAP apps to meet specific customer requirements. For detailed guidance, check out our documentation on leveraging SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in the SAP Business Application Studio.

Image 1: Template Wizard in SAP Business Application with SAPUI5 Adaptation Project Tile

 

Updated HDI Deployer in SAP HANA Projects

We’ve upgraded the SAP HANA Deployment Infrastructure Deployer (HDI deployer) in the “SAP HANA Database Project” template from version 4 to 5. This new version streamlines project setups by removing the built-in SAP HANA client, reducing overhead and simplifying client management.

While the generation of new projects has already been adjusted, if you're upgrading existing projects, make sure to manually include the SAP HANA client in your project dependencies to avoid disruptions.

Image 2: Manual insertion of SAP HANA Client in existing project

To assist you with the transition, SAP Business Application Studio now includes a problem indicator for projects using HDI deployer version 5 without an SAP HANA client. This problem indicator not only helps to discover incomplete configurations it also offers a quick-fix action for easy resolution.

Image 3: Problem Indicator for SAP HANA client dependency in SAP Business Application Studio

We encourage all developers to upgrade to this latest version to benefit from the newest features and innovations.

 

Discover how to extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build Code

Have you heard about SAP Build Code? It’s our latest offering on SAP BTP, tailored for developers working with SAPUI5, SAP Fiori elements and CAP. It offers a turn-key development environment that integrates runtime and design time capabilities, facilitating coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. SAP Build Code includes Joule, a generative AI copilot, enhancing developer productivity by automating the generation of application logic, data models, and testing scripts, allowing for a smoother and faster development process. 

Discover more about this innovative solution by watching this video (https://youtu.be/py5AST8UGe4?si=LgjWBVWJ1g8KvSbF) or dive deeper into transitioning from the SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code in this insightful blog post by Rupa Maity.

 

Influence SAP products and join our SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives

  • SAP S/4HANA Extensibility using SAP Build, SAP Build Code and ABAP
    Shape the future of enterprise applications with the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for SAP Build and SAP Build Code. This initiative offers a unique opportunity to collaborate on developing Extension Scenarios and Extension Applications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud systems in Side-by-Side mode. By participating, you’ll engage directly with the latest SAP technologies for  Process Extensions, Application Extensions, Mobile Extensions, and ABAP-based Extensions, as well as advanced Cloud API consumption scenarios. Help us tailor these tools to your real-life business needs and use cases, ensuring they serve you better in driving efficient, integrated development across your enterprise environments.
  • SAPUI5 2.x
    Are you looking to adapt your app to SAPUI5 2.x? Join our upcoming Customer Engagement Initiative project and influence how applications evolve with SAPUI5 2.x. For more details and to register, read this blog post by Oliver Graeff and make your mark!

 

For further information on new features, please have a look at the “What’s new” information for SAP Business Application Studio and check our roadmap. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding ! 😊

entry.title: Re: Missing Product Hierachy in S4H Public Cloud 2208 Man...
entry.content:
Hello

In an On-Premise System , the Product Hierachy is maintained through the customizing table T179 (field MARA-PRDHA on the Basic Data view for a Material ) but in S/4HANA Cloud Product Hierarchy is implemented differently and outlined in the blog below and not available in the Manage Product Master data MM01 / MM02 / MM03 apps

https://blogs.sap.com/2019/11/19/introduction-to-product-hierarchy-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/

The application "Manage Product Master Data" will only display the relevant hierarchy in the "Distribution Chain" page. This display is possible only for those product hierarchies which are assigned to distribution chains using the application "Manage Product Hierarchy Assignments" (App ID: F4240)

entry.title: Re: How can we assign MIC in breakdown maintenance order
entry.content:
Greetings,

I don't think you can assign inspection characteristics to maintenance order directly but you could try maintaining a task list with predefined inspection data.

Before you release the order you could include this task list in the maintenance order via Extras -> Task list selection -> Direct entry. You should get an inspection lot if your order type has inspection type assigned to it.

This would mean that you predefine inspection characteristics. You can always assign additional during results recording as unplanned characteristics.

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: Resuming a subscription is not possible
entry.content:
Hi Alberto, 

in order to avoid complex time-zone calculations on the service consumer side, you can use the parameter billing-sb-dates-in-subscription-timezone to send only local dates. Otherwise you would need to consider e.g. daylight saving time on your own.
If you set this parameter, then the dates should be in local date format. 
See also the description for this parameter:

Set this parameter to true if you want to provide dates in local date-time without reference to a time zone.
The service determines the correct time zone from the field subscriptionTimeZone, and if this field is not filled, from the market time zone.
If this parameter is not set, you need to provide the dates in the UTC time zone.
Use a date yyyy-mm-dd to achieve a time stamp at midnight in the subscription time zone.


Therefore, adjust your payload accordingly (assuming the resume date should be Jun 25th 2024):

 

{
    "metaData": {
        "version": 3
    },
    "pauseSchedule": [
        {
            "pauseDate": "2023-06-28",
            "resumeDate": "2024-06-25",            
            "reason": "MAN"
        }
    ]
}

 

Best
Carsten

entry.title: Re: Time Zone Adjustment for SAP Cloud ALM Operation Modu...
entry.content:
KBA 3337151 has the full details about this topic. 

In short, timestamps are stored in the SAP Cloud ALM backend as "raw" UTC values.

Some data (and their timestamps) are considered relevant to the operation of SAP Cloud ALM itself, such as the time an alert was generated or an automatic e-mail notification sent. Those values are converted to the User's system time zone, and displayed in the web interfaces / e-mails.

Other data are considered service/system specific, and it has been decided to use "raw" UTC in the display of the timestamps. This is useful for Customers operating many services/systems across different time zones/locations. For example, it is useful for use cases Integration & Exception Monitoring and Business Process Monitoring. Those use cases involve several services/systems working in tandem as part of a larger process. How they integrate together is important. So if there is an error that may touch multiple services/systems (each possibly operating in different time zones), it is useful to have a "unified" way of looking at timestamps.

Currently SAP Cloud ALM offers no setting to "force" the conversion of these raw UTC values to the user's device time zone.

If you want to influence the direction of the development of SAP Cloud ALM, particularly in the context of this matter, I suggest you leverage the SAP Continuous Influence programs for SAP Cloud ALM: 3391676

entry.title: Re: Which user should I place in the SAP Corporate Group...
entry.content:
You mentioned an error message, but didn't include a screen shot of it or the text that described the error. We're missing some important context, so it makes responding a little difficult.

The "SAP Corporate Group (CCC) S-User" area in the Landscape Management app should only be filled if you are trying to import Cloud Services associated with another Customer number in your CCC group.

As per KBA 3070306, the user should be a technical communication user for the CCC, not an Administrator.

More detailed information is listed in that KBA, including the prerequisites etc.

entry.title: Architecting Complex Real-Time Business Solutions: SAP BPA with dynamic Multi level Approval
entry.content:
This is the third blog in our series where my colleague, Mohit Bansal, and I discuss architecture, solutioning, and real-time workflow scenarios. In our previous blog we discussed about "Architecting Complex Real-Time Business Solutions: SAP Build Process Automation with Auto Approval"

In this part, we will delve into complex workflows with multi-level approval.

Understand Business Requirements & Problem statement:

Before We Dive into the Business Requirements let’s understand with real time Business scenario. In many organizations, there’s a complex process for approving financial budgets. This process involves various levels of approval based on factors like company code, cost center, and the total amount. Here’s how it works:

  1. Complex Delegation of Authorities:
    • Imagine a scenario where different people have the authority to approve different types of expenses. For example, a manager might approve small expenses, while a director handles larger ones.
    • This delegation of authority ensures that the right person approves each request.
  2. Multiple Levels of Approval:
    • The approval process isn’t straightforward. It can involve several levels, from team leads to department heads to executives.
    • The number of approval levels isn’t fixed—it can vary based on the specific situation.
  3. Example:
    • Suppose an employee submits a budget request for a new project. The request includes details like the project’s cost center (which department it belongs to) and the total amount needed.
    • The approval workflow kicks in: First, the team lead reviews and approves it. Then, it goes to the department head. If the amount is substantial, it might even reach the CEO for final approval.

Technical Design & Solution:

Let’s understand the technical flow which we will be developing in SAP Build process automation.

Workflow Trigger (step 1A, 1B & 1C):

  • To kick off our SAP Build Process automation-based workflow, we have several options:
    • Form Trigger: Users can initiate the workflow by filling out a form with relevant details.
    • API Trigger: An external system or application can trigger the workflow via an API call.
    • Event Trigger: Specific events (e.g., data changes, system events) can automatically start the workflow.
  • We’ll use process variables to determine the decision matrix. These variables might include company code, cost center, amount, and approval levels.
Approver Determination (Steps 2 & 3):appropriate number of decision matrix entries.

Let’s illustrate this with an real time scenario as below:

Company CodeCost CenterAmountCurrencyLevelApprover ID
1000CC0120000USD1JohnDoe
1000CC0120000USD2Alex
1000CC0120000USD3Frank
1001CC0230000USD1Kevin
1001CC0230000USD2Peter

Define the Decision input Parameters : Company Code, Cost Center, Amount,   Currency, & Transaction Type.  

Define the Decision table based business conditions. it can be compared with the approval table which we have defined above.
 

Automation Step & Increment the counter (step 4 & 5).

This step will be used to count the number of approver level in step number 2.

It will take initial counter as 0 and will increment the counter to send to next level via script task.

 

 

var iCount = 0;
if (oApprovers.iCurrentIteration == null || oApprovers.iCurrentIteration == 
undefined){
    iCount = 0;}
else{
    iCount = oApprovers.iCurrentIteration + 1;
}

return {
    "NoofLevels" : oApprovers.aApprovers.length,
    "Approver" : oApprovers.aApprovers[iCount].Approver,
    "currentIterationCount": iCount
};

 

 

It is important to note that, we have set the output parameter as number of level subtracting 1.

This is required as index for approval array is starting from ZERO.  

We need to bind custom variables with Automation’s Input along with decision table output. Similarly, Automation’s output will be mapped to Custom variable.

Approval Step & Loop Go to Step ( Step 6 ,7 & 😎

We will send the Approval step to respective Approver based on the correct authorities retrieved for that specific level.

Here, we need to set the current approver dynamically retrieved from the Automation Step.

and check if we have reached highest level of approval in condition.

And here is the final flow our complete workflow:

Conclusion Remarks :

My colleague Mohit Bansal and I have devised an innovative approach to dynamically handle multilevel approvals. Our solution can be easily scaled to meet your specific requirements, empowering you to design complex delegation authorities with ease.

We believe this method will significantly enhance your approval workflows and streamline decision-making processes.

Thank you for reading, and happy automating! 

Please feel free to get in touch with me over Linkedin.

Regards,

Adwait Fadnavis,

SAP BTP Consultant

entry.title: Re: How to activate the following services in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
The built-in help functionality clarifies what each status means in the Landscape Management app:

In short, those statuses represent the interaction between the service/system and SAP Cloud ALM, not the status of the actual service/system itself. To move the state to "Active", you have to add those systems to the "scope" of one of the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations use case apps (e.g. Health Monitoring, etc.) and enable the data collection.

entry.title: What is planned for the 2408 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing?
entry.content:
The 2408 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing is planned to be deployed to BTP customer tenants on July 24, 2024.

This blog covers the following planned innovations:

  • Pricing service – Condition Exclusion on Condition Record Level
  • Pricing service – Consider Cost Conditions as External Conditions
  • Pricing service – Standard Requirement Formula 61
  • Pricing service – Overwriting the Used Exchange Rate
  • Pricing service – Return the Scales Order
  • Pricing Service – Return Condition Scales in Stateless Pricing
  • Variant Configuration Service – Do Not Process Inactive Knowledge Bases
  • Variant Configuration Service – Consider Locks for all Knowledge Bases
  • Variant Configuration Service – Filter Invisible Characteristics
  • Variant Configuration Service – Select Variant Table Content
  • Variant Configuration Service – Manage Stored Configurations
  • Extension Concept – Restricted Syntax in Local Extensions
  • Extensions Concept – New APIs to Test Local Extensions
  • Extensions Concept – New APIs to Upload and Activate Local Extensions
  • Extensions Concept - Display Active JavaScript Functions
  • Administration – Deprecation of The Table KONH
  • Administration – Parallel Delta Replication with Multiple Remote Sources
  • Administration – Upgrade Tables Without Downtime
  • Administration – Warnings for High Delta Load

 

It also includes a call to act regarding the following potentially incompatible changes:

  • Variant Configuration Service – Do Not Process Inactive Knowledge Bases
  • Variant Configuration Service – Consider Locks for all Knowledge Bases
  • Administration – Deprecation of The Table KONH

 

Please check the updated roadmap and release notes after the release date to see which of these features was delivered as planned.

 

Disclaimer

The information in this presentation is confidential and proprietary to SAP and may not be disclosed without the permission of SAP.
Except for your obligation to protect confidential information, this presentation is not subject to your license agreement or any other service or subscription agreement with SAP. SAP has no obligation to pursue any course of business outlined in this presentation or any related document, or to develop or release any functionality mentioned therein.

This presentation, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this presentation is not a commitment, promise or legal obligation to deliver any material, code or functionality.  This presentation is provided without a warranty of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. This presentation is for informational purposes and may not be incorporated into a contract. SAP assumes no responsibility for errors or omissions in this presentation, except if such damages were caused by SAP’s intentional or gross negligence.

All forward-looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from expectations. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which speak only as of their dates,
and they should not be relied upon in making purchasing decisions.

 

Pricing service – Condition Exclusion on Condition Record Level

The exclusion indicator (KONP-KZNEP) that is maintained for a condition record will be considered during the condition determination of  the next condition types in a pricing procedure.

For that, the following standard requirement formulas will be changed:

  • Requirement formula 5 is now supported. It ensures that a condition type is only considered if the exclusion indicator was not set by a previously found condition record.
  • Requirement formula 6 is now supported. It ensures that a condition type is only considered if the exclusion indicator was not set to ‘X’ by a previously found condition record.
  • Requirement formula 2 was enhanced to not only check if the pricing indicator (KOMP-PRSFD) equals X or B, but also to check that the exclusion indicator was not set by a previously found condition record.

Example:

A pricing procedure with requirement formulas 2, 6 and 5:

Example Pricing Procedure

For the pricing condition ZZPR we have maintained a condition record where exclusion indicator was set to ‘X’:

Condition Record with Exclusion Indicator

In this example, upon successfully determining this condition record for the condition type ZZPR, no further condition determination will occur for the subsequent condition types (K005, K006, and K009). This is because the requirement formula will not be fulfilled as explained in the bullet points above, resulting in the subsequent conditions being ignored.

More information will be published in Supported Standard Pricing Exits.

 

Pricing service – Consider Cost Conditions as External Conditions

Condition types with data source other than Condition Technique are considered as external conditions where the condition  rate must be passed by the caller if it shall be considered.

What is new is that we will also consider cost condition types without an access sequence and with condition category Q (Cost) and G (Internal price) as external conditions, as the data for condition types like EK01, EK02, and VPRS cannot be found in condition records, but must be passed by the service consumer.

Reminder: GET /pricingprocedure/{pricing procedure id} tells you which condition types are considered as external conditions:

 

{
  "attributeNames": [
    "KOMK-HWAER",
    "KOMK-KONDA",
    "KOMK-KUNNR",
    "KOMK-PLTYP",
    "KOMK-VKORG",
    "KOMK-VTWEG",
    "KOMK-WAERK",
    "KOMP-KONDM",
    "KOMP-PMATN",
    "KOMP-PRSFD",
    "KOMP-VARCOND"
  ],
  "subProcedureDeterminationParameterIds": [
    "KOMK-ALAND",
    "KOMK-LAND1",
    "KOMK-TAXCONDITIONDETNCTRYREG"
  ],
  "accessDateList": [
    "KOMK-FBUDA",
    "KOMK-PRSDT"
  ],
  "externalConditions": [
    {
      "conditionTypeName": "ZPRI",
      "conditionTypeDescription": "External Price",
      "dataSource": "G",
      "dataSourceDescription": "EBP: Price From Product Catalog"
    },
    {
      "conditionTypeName": "ZEDI",
      "conditionTypeDescription": "External Discount",
      "dataSource": "E",
      "dataSourceDescription": "Cash Discount"
    },
    {
      "conditionTypeName": "ZTAX",
      "conditionTypeDescription": "External Tax",
      "dataSource": "C",
      "dataSourceDescription": "Tax Determination"
    }
  ]
}

 

Example of how to fill such external conditions when creating a pricing document item:

POST /api/v1/pricing/documents/{documentId}/items

 

{
  "items": [
    {
      "externalConditions": [
        {
          "calculationType": "B",
          "conditionTypeId": "ZEDI",
          "conditionTypeName": "Flat 2 USD discount.",
          "rate": {
            "unit": "USD",
            "value": -2
          }
        },
        {
          "calculationType": "A",
          "conditionTypeId": "ZTAX",
          "rate": {
            "unit": "P1",
            "value": 19
          }
        }
      ],

 

See also API Definition.

 

Pricing service – Standard Requirement Formula 61

Standard requirement formula 61 will be supported, it checks if the pricing indicator (KOMP-PRSFD) equals X (Pricing standard) or B (Pricing for free goods (100% discount)) and that the item is not statistical. An item is considered as statistical when the item field statistical is passed as true when calling the Pricing service.

More information will be published in Supported Standard Pricing Exits with the August release.

 

Pricing service – Overwriting the Used Exchange Rate

By passing the pricing attribute KOMP-KURSK, it will be possible to overwrite the item’s used exchange rate when converting the local currency to the document currency.

See also API Definition.

 

Pricing service – Return the Scales Order

The returned condition type information will be enhanced with the new field scaleOrder, that tells the caller whether the scale rates must be passed in ascending or descending order.

The following endpoints will be enhanced for this:

  • GET /api/v1/customizing/procedures/{pricingProcedureId}
  • GET /api/v1/customizing/procedures/{pricingProcedureId}/listView
  • GET /api/v1/customizing/conditiontypes/{conditionTypeId}
  • GET /api/v1/pricing/documents/{documentId}/items/{itemId}/conditions/{conditionId}

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Development Guide with the August release.

 

Pricing Service – Return Condition Scales in Document Pricing

When creating condition records in the back end, scales can be maintained. The Pricing service determines the right scale value, uses it in price calculation and returns the corresponding price, discount, or surcharge. For example, lower prices are used for higher item quantities.

Since the last release, scales for item conditions are returned by all relevant endpoints of document pricing. For more information see here.

With the next release, we will also enhance stateless pricing endpoint to return the scale information (including the scales order):

  • POST /api/v1/statelesspricing

The new $select-parameter value items-conditions-scale that can be passed by the caller to additionally request the scale information will be introduced:

$select=items-conditions-scale

 

The item conditions response structure will be enhanced to return the scale information as already known from document pricing.

Example:

 

"conditions": [
  {
    "stepNo": 11,
    "conditionCounter": 1,
    "conditionType": "PR00",
    "conditionTypeDescription": "Price",
    "purpose": "BASE",
    "conditionBase": "1.000",
    "conditionRate": "3.98",
    "conditionCurrency": "EUR",
    "conditionUnitValue": "1.000",
    "conditionUnit": "PCE",
    "conditionValue": "3.98",
    "statistical": false,
    "calculationType": "C",
    "varcond": false,
    "varcondKey": null,
    "varcondFactor": null,
    "durationFactor": null,
    "inactiveFlag": " ",
    "conditionRateConverted": "3.98",
    "conditionRateNormalized": "3.98",
    "conditionTable": "A304",
    "scale": {
      "scaleType": "A",
      "scaleBaseType": "C",
      "scaleOrder": "A",
      "rateQuantity": {
        "value": 1.0,
        "unit": "ST"
      },
      "scaleUnit": "ST",
      "levels": [
        {
          "id": 1,
          "scaleValue": "1.000",
          "scaleRate": {
            "value": "3.98",
            "unit": "EUR"
          },
          "scaleRateConverted": {
            "value": "3.98",
            "unit": "EUR"
          }
        },
        {
          "id": 4,
          "scaleValue": "5.000",
          "scaleRate": {
            "value": "3.22",
            "unit": "EUR"
          },
          "scaleRateConverted": {
            "value": "3.22",
            "unit": "EUR"
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  }
]

 

More information will be published in the API Definition with the August release.

 

Variant Configuration Service – Do Not Process Inactive Knowledge Bases

During knowledge-base generation, when a new runtime version is created or an existing version is re-generated (new build), first the knowledge-base version will be flagged as inactive in the header table, then all other tables will be updated, and finally, it will be flagged as active again in the header table.

Currently, this is only done in the case of classic knowledge-base generation when variant tables exist. In the future, it is planned to apply the same behavior also when no variant tables exist and for the advanced knowledge-base generation. This shall ensure transactional consistency when the data is replicated from the back end to the Variant Configuration service.

Until now, the Variant Configuration service used to consider the active flag only in the endpoint for searching knowledge bases: GET /api/v2/kbdetermination.

Inactive knowledge bases are not returned by this endpoint.

But when creating new configurations with a specified knowledge-base id (KBID), or when changing or copying an existing configuration, it was not checked if the used knowledge-base runtime version was still active.

This will change with the August release: An inactive knowledge-base runtime version indicates inconsistent data and shall not be loaded. The Variant Configuration service will return an error response if a configuration shall be processed based on an inactive knowledge-base version. Before returning the error, the service will retry the last action several times during about 30 seconds before finally returning the error. As soon as the knowledge-base generation and the data replication are completed, the configuration can be processed again.

Action:

  • It is important to have a governance process in place about how to update product models including knowledge bases and how to publish model changes without impacting ongoing configurations. See also Best Practices to Generate Knowledge-Base Runtime Versions.

 

Variant Configuration Service – Consider Locks for all Knowledge Bases

When a knowledge-base runtime version is replicated from the back end into the cloud, the knowledge-base version is flagged as locked as long as some tables are not yet completely replicated.

The lock is set by the replication process within the administration UI of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing.

The active flag mentioned in the previous chapter is set by the knowledge-base generation in the back end when the knowledge-base creation or update is processed there.

Until now, the Variant Configuration service used to consider the lock only when a classical knowledge base with variant tables was replicated.

This will change with the August release: A locked knowledge base indicates inconsistent data and shall not be loaded. The Variant Configuration service will return an error response if a configuration shall be processed based on a locked knowledge-base version. Before returning the error, the service will retry the last action several times during about 30 seconds before finally returning the error. The lock will be considered for both, classical and advanced knowledge-base generation, independent of whether variant tables are contained or not. As soon as the knowledge-base replication is completed, the configuration can be processed again.

Action:

  • It is important to have a governance process in place about how to update product models including knowledge bases and how to publish model changes without impacting ongoing configurations. See also Best Practices to Generate Knowledge-Base Runtime Versions.

 

Variant Configuration Service – Filter Invisible Characteristics

A new request parameter $filter will be introduced for the endpoint GET /api/v2/configurations/{configurationId}, that allows reducing the response size by omitting the invisible characteristics that are usually not needed in the consuming application:

$filter=characteristicVisible EQ true

Action:

  • If your application does not need to process invisible characteristics, it is recommended to apply the new query parameter $filter=characteristicVisible EQ true to reduce the response payload.

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Development Guide with the August release.

 

Variant Configuration Service – Select Variant Table Content

A new request parameter $filter will be introduced for the endpoint GET /knowledgebases/{kbId}/variantTables/{variantTableId} that allows specifying characteristic names and values to select the desired table content.

$filter=<column name> EQ <characteristic value>

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Development Guide with the August release.

 

Variant Configuration Service – Manage Stored Configurations

When creating a new configuration with endpoint POST /configurations, it will be possible to specify the owner or the source of the configuration: The source application (for example cpq), source type (for example quote_item) and source id (for example item number 10) can be passed and stored with the configuration.

Example call:

POST /configurations

 

{
  "context": [],
  "date": "2018-08-09",
  "kbId": 80,
  "productKey": "CPS_BURGER",
  "source": {
    "application": "cpq",
    "type": "quote_item",
    "id": "10"
  }
}

 

The source of a configuration can also be specified in the same way when creating a new configuration through endpoint POST /externalConfigurations.

Action:

  • It is recommended to specify the source of the configuration, so that it can later be used to identify the owner of the configuration and to judge if it can be deleted again.

The new endpoint GET /externalConfigurations will be introduced to return a list of stored configurations. The API will offer different filter capabilities, for example filtering based on the source of the configuration.

A stored configuration can be read with endpoint GET /externalConfigurations/{configurationId}/display.

A stored configuration can be deleted with endpoint DELETE /configurations/{configurationId}.

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Development Guide with the August release.

 

Extensions Concept – Restricted Syntax in Local Extensions

Since the May release, when running local extensions, the following syntax is no longer allowed:

  • Keywords: "async", "await", generator functions ("function*")
  • Functions(*): "eval", "load", "loadWithNewGlobal", "print", "printErr"
  • Objects(*): "console", "Graal", "FinalizationRegistry", "Function", "Promise", "Proxy", "Reflect", "Symbol"

(*)Using them will cause a runtime error.

With the August release, this will already be checked when uploading the extensions code to configuration and pricing services.

This helps to identify such problems early on and avoid tedious error analysis later.

For more information see the Extension Guide.

 

Extensions Concept – New APIs to Test Local Extensions

Currently, local extension implementations can only be tested by calling the Pricing service or Variant Configuration service and checking the expected pricing or configuration results.

We will introduce new endpoints that allow testing the active custom routines directly:

  • GET /functions: Returns the list of active uploaded JavaScript functions and flags those that are executable pricing routines or variant functions.
  • POST /functions/{functionId}: Allows to process an active uploaded pricing routine or variant function.

The use for the POST endpoint will be blocked for productive tenants!

Please leave us a comment below if you tested those new endpoints and let us know if they add value to your extension implementation and test processes.

More information will be published in the API Definition, the Development Guide and the Extension Guide with the August release.

 

Extensions Concept – New APIs to Upload and Activate Local Extensions

Currently, it is only possible to upload and activate JavaScript archives for local extensions through the administration UI.

With the August release, we will introduce a new service called Administration service with the following APIs:

  • POST /localExtension/v1/archives: Allows to upload a local extensions JavaScript archive file.
  • GET /localExtension/v1/archives: Returns the list of uploaded archive files.
  • GET /localExtension/v1/archives/{guid}/content: Allows downloading a file again.
  • DELETE /localExtension/v1/archives: Allows to delete an uploaded file.
  • PATCH /localExtension/v1/archives: Allows the activation of an uploaded file.

With that, and the new test APIs for local extensions mentioned above, it will be possible to implement local extensions and use those new APIs to automate uploads, activations, and tests.

More information will be published in the Feature Scope Description and the Extension Guide with the August release.

 

Extensions Concept - Display Active JavaScript Functions

We will introduce a new section ‘Active Extensions’ on the screen to upload and activate JavaScript archives for local extensions:

Active JavaScript Functions

The new section provides a list of all active JavaScript functions and flags those as executable that implement custom pricing routines or variant functions.

For that, the administration UI uses the new API GET /functions mentioned above.

 

Administration – Deprecation of The Table KONH

The Pricing service does not need the conditions header table KONH. The table is no longer loaded since the May release. The table will be removed with the August release 2408.

Action:

  • Please check whether you are using the table in any local extension implementation. If that is the case, it must be added to the replication as a custom table.

See also the Administration Guide.

 

Administration – Parallel Delta Replication with Multiple Remote Sources

Currently, when replicating data from the back end using SAP HANA smart data integration (SDI), we use one remote source to connect the cloud service to the back end. This has the disadvantage that when knowledge bases are replicated, pricing data must wait, and vice versa.

With the August release, we will introduce a new option to distribute the replication on two so-called remote sources; one for configuration and one for pricing-related tables.

Remote Source Settings

This will allow us to run those delta replications in parallel, which will on the one hand reduce the overall time needed for the delta replications and on the other hand it ensures that pricing updates do not block configuration updates, and vice versa.

More information will be published in the Administration Guide with the August release. 

 

Administration – Upgrade Tables Without Downtime

Applying dictionary changes to replicated tables in the source systems, for example during release updates, can cause replication errors. Currently, those can only be resolved with a downtime.

This will be improved, so that the Upgrade Table functionality found under Monitoring -> Tables will be done in a way, that it will not cause any downtimes for the connected configuration and pricing services.

Upgrade Table

 

Administration – Warnings for High Delta Load

Several improvements will be done to inform on a high number of pending deltas. There can be a high number of deltas, when doing mass updates in the source system like updating many prices or updating many knowledge bases. The delta replication can process approximately 25 changes per second. This is much slower as the full initial replication which achieves a throughput of about 10, 000 rows per second or more. This means, there is a sweet spot where it is faster to do a full load instead of a delta replication. To be aware of a running delta, the following improvements have been done:

  1. Replication Status Header:
    The delta replication tile is showing an error in case of a too high delta load:
    Delta Load Too High

     

  2. A warning is added on the top of the screen:
    Warning Message

     

With that, you have a good overview of what innovations are planned for the August release. Please check the updated roadmap and release notes after the release date to see which of these features was delivered as planned.

entry.title: Re: Alv grid (oops):hiding empty column
entry.content:
DATA(where) = |{ ls_fieldcat-fieldname } IS NOT INITIAL|.
      LOOP AT gt_alv INTO DATA(ls_alv) WHERE (where).
      ENDLOOP.
      IF sy-subrc NE 0.
        ls_fcat-no_out = abap_true.
      ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud for Utilities foundation, or the Measurement Concept Management
entry.content:
Hello @TillHeinen , thank you for your question.


SAP Event Mesh is currently still available via the commercial model “Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement” (CPEA) and existing customers can use it until the end of the CPEA contracts. New customer can buy the “old” event mesh, too.

Going forward, Event Mesh will move into SAP Integration Suite and will be included as a capability.

At some point in time, latest when the customer’s CPEA contract will end, customers have to migrate to Event Mesh within Integration Suite or

to the advanced event mesh.

My assumption is that you can not subscribe event mesh in subaccount1 because the corresponding global account uses the commercial model

“Pay-as-you-go (PAYG)”.
Please be aware that event mesh needs to be subscribed in the same subaccount as MCM.

 

If you have further questions, please let us know!


Best regards

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2406 Release – What’s New?
entry.content:
This blog post continues my monthly series on SAP Business Network for Logistics release highlights and gives you an overview of the 2406 release. The blog is intended for shippers and solution owners, carriers, and data providers as well as sales and presales colleagues who already have a detailed understanding of SAP Business Network for Logistics. 

Before we dive deeper into the 2406 release highlights, I would like to point you to the ‘in-between’ release we had on May 31st, 2024. This special release marks an important milestone. As our new functions around managing your business profile are the first evidence of the convergence of the previously separate networks in the areas of procurement and supply chain collaboration, assets, and logistics.

From now on, maintenance of company profile and onboarding of carriers and data contributors will be done on the new platform serving as the foundation of the SAP Business Network. This is the starting point for many integration scenarios that our customers are waiting for.

 

There is a separate blog post on the May ‘in-between’ release. Make sure you read it to learn more around this exciting change!

... back to the 2406 release.

Cross SAP Business Network for Logistics

New integration platform for SAP Cloud Integration enabled for test tenants

In April, we announced that the Neo environment on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) has been deprecated. As a result, you need to migrate your existing connections to the new integration platform hosted in SAP BTP, multi-cloud environment. For a recap, please read this blog.

Now, we enabled the migration to the new integration platform in your test tenants.

From the 2406 release onwards when shippers create a connection in their ‘System Connections’ app (test tenant), the connection will now point to the new integration tenant and not the old one. This means that the SAP Business Network for Logistics destination URL that they need to maintain in their backend SAP system will be a new one. Outbound cloud connector configurations would also differ as the new integration tenant needs to be added in the customer’s cloud connector instead of the old one. Customers can continue using the existing connections until the sunset (announced as May 16, 2025) and no impact should happen for any current integrations. Please note, that at any point in this migration activity shippers should not delete their existing connections.

Carrier specific changes are minimal. Carriers might need to enhance the whitelisting/allow-listing as the new platforms are hosted in a different data center. Details regarding the same are also linked in their ‘System Connections’ app.

Please migrate to the new integration platform as soon as possible to see if any incompatibilities or issues arise. This will help us resolve potential issues before enabling migration to the productive tenants.

Detailed steps to migrate are described in the following guides:

  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Shippers and Solution Owners (applicable for Freight Collaboration, Global Track and Trace and Intelligent Insights)
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Carriers and Data Contributors (applicable for Freight Collaboration, Global Track and Trace and Intelligent Insights)

Migration changes will be made available for productive tenants in an upcoming release.

Enhancement to ‘Consumer API for Order Tracking’

In the ‘Consumer API for Order Tracking’ (FC) and ‘Consumer API for Order Tracking’ (GTT), the field "ISORegionCode" is added to standardize the way of specifying the region of stops in a shipment. The values will be populated from the list of ISO 3166-2.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Contracting: Vendor ID added as filter criteria and worklist item

Filtering and displaying of the vendor ID was already available in the ‘Freight Order for Confirmation’ and ‘Freight Order for Reporting’ applications. Now, the vendor ID was also added as filter criteria and worklist item in the carrier applications ‘Freight Request for Quotation’ and ‘Freight Quotations’.

 Please note, the vendor ID was also added to the ‘Consumer API for Freight Tendering’.

Dock appointment scheduling: Changes in default settings for new loading points

‘Enable Time Slot Setting’ for new loading points in the ‘Manage Entities’ application is now disabled per default.

Settlement: New validation checks for ‘Provider API for Invoicing’

When carriers submit an invoice via the ‘Provider API for Invoicing’, there are now multiple validation checks enabled (resulting in an error or warning):

  • Rate amount and final amount must be the same for unplanned charge
  • Rate amount currency and final amount currency must be the same for unplanned charge
  • Quantity will be ignored for an unplanned charge
  • Percentage rate is not allowed for an unplanned charge
  • Invoice amount cannot be less than zero
  • Final amount cannot be zero for charge lines
  • Tax amount cannot be negative
  • Sum of all the pricing elements and item taxes is not equal to the item gross amount
  • Sum of all the tax amount not matching with total tax amount

Settlement: Enhancement to ‘Provider API for Invoicing’

For invoices created via ‘Provider API for Invoicing’, you can now see the item details.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Built-in support modernized

The built-in support has been enabled on the Fiori launchpad of SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace. It provides an integrated and modernized support experience. You can now enjoy the following key services without leaving the launchpad and applications:

  • Search for information in multiple SAP support knowledge bases, such as SAP Help Portal, SAP Notes, SAP Community, or SAP Help Portal.
  • Report issues to SAP and review your issues.
  • View a list of recommended helpful documentation links.

To gain full access to all built-in support services, you must activate your S-user, following the steps described in Activating Built-In Support Services.

 

Please share and amplify our new features!

---------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2406. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2406 release can be found in the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: Re: What are possible categories for integration monitoring?

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Bangalore, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps and business processes without code (Low Code/No Code). 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app for an online store (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process for approving purchases (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • We will also demo a business site in which you can embed the app (SAP Build Work Zone).

You will get to know the SAP Build lobby, where you access all your SAP Build artifacts, and get a deeper understanding of the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
    • Connecting to an ABAP, S/4HANA  and CAP service
    • Building UIs
    • Binding data
    • Navigation
    • Triggering processes from your app
    • Scanning barcodes and implementing other mobile capabilities
    • Filtering data
  • SAP Build Process Automation: 
    • Creating a process
    • Adding approvals
    • Implementing a rework scenario
    • Creating actions
    • Calling SAP backends (SAP S/4HANA Cloud and CAP services)
    • Updating data
    • Conditions and GoTo flows
    • Custom variables

entry.title: Re: BTMF Issue in SOM
entry.content:
Hi,

The customizing node that is mentioned above (BMTF -> Activate Change Process -> Define Transaction Type in Process) does not play a role in determining whether a change process is available for execution or not. This customizing is meant to define what should be the Transaction Type of the Change Order that will be created upon execution of the change process. As an example you could have two possible Transaction Types defined for Change Order and then in the copy control settings of ‘Subscription Contract to Change Order’, for one you could have the setting as ‘do not copy’ and for the other ‘copy all conditions’ with regards to the price conditions, and then attach the Transaction Types in this customizing to the change processes depending on whether you need a fresh pricing in the Change Order or not.

 

With regards to how to control the availability of a change process there are two options

  • Define and attach a BRF+ Function at the ‘Define Change Process’ node (In the BRF+ Function you could read the Transaction Type and then decide whether the process that the function is triggered for is allowed or not) or
  • Create and attach a new class (inheriting from the standard class) to the change process at the ‘Define Change Process’ node and in the redefined IF_CRM_BTMF~CHECK method of the new class code based on any parameters on whether the process is allowed or not

Thanks,
Vinay

entry.title: How to allow any external SSCC number for HUs while still being able to generate them internally?
entry.content:
Hi

We want to be able to receive pallets and packages in one plant without having to re-label them, so we want to be able to use their SSCC codes as our handling unit ID. 

We have not found a way to allow this in HU02 while still having our current setup for internal SSCC code generation (using "Maintain SSCC Generation for Each Plant / Storage Location" in SPRO). 

The issue is that the packaging material type in MM02 for our materials (EU Pallets, etc) are not plant specific, so if we change the properties of the packaging material type it messes with the HU ID generation in all plants.

entry.title: Re: Seeking Assistance with Fiori ListReport App Grid Tab...
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

Update: I might have found something. It seems like the HTML code for the checkboxes and classes for the table header isn't being generated. However, the multi-select setting exists in the deployed app version.

For anyone experiencing a similar issue, I've implemented this workaround in my controller:

onAfterRendering: function (oEvent) {
// Workaround for multi-select
var oTable = this._getTable();
oTable._getSelectionPlugin().setSelectionMode('MultiToggle');
},
_getTable: function () {
return this.byId("YourGridTable");
}

I'd appreciate any ideas on how to solve this problem more elegantly.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
Introduction

I have experimented a bit with SAP Cloud Transport Management.
I would like to share my experiences in this blog post.

The Big Picture

As usual on SAP BTP you have to use a service from the service marketplace to use the SAP Cloud Transport Management. You also have to create a solution landscape on the SAP BTP. 

I designed it as follows:

SAP Cloud Transport Management

The application looks likt this:

Transport Nodes

I created Transport Nodes for every System, like my 3 system landscape DEV, QAS, PRD.

Transport Routes

Very similar to the transport routes in classic ABAP.

Landscape Visualization

I really like the visualization with the status of the import queues and it gives a good overview.

Integration with SAP Cloud ALM

For a comprehensive SAP ALM, it is important that SAP Cloud Transport Management can also be connected to SAP Cloud ALM. This means that the entire life cycle from the process, the requirement, testing to deployment and operation can be managed. Here a useful blogpost: Link

 

From my point of view, the following points are important for Transport Management/Deloyments

  • The connection of hybrid landscapes
  • The central control of deployments
  • Orchestrating multiple system landscapes
  • Deployments of hybrid development technologies
  • Central, clear reporting
  • Options for intervention in the event of errors
  • Integration with SAP Cloud ALM

entry.content:
Hello Everyone, 

as you probably noticed - Classic Design Experience in People Analytics Story will be deprecated and customers have to convert their reports created in CDE to Optimized Design Experience. 

One of limitations of Optimized Design Experience is that it doesn't support blending data in tables/charts. 

Many customers use the blending data feature in their stories. And after conversion from CDE to ODE the tables/chars that used blending data become useful. 

So the questions are: Is there any workaround for that? Is SAP planning to enable the blending data feature in Optimized Design Experience before deprecation of Classic Design Experience?


Best regards

Lukasz

entry.title: Adding Custom Metrics to a CAP application using cap-js/telementry plugin and SAP Cloud Logging
entry.content:
About

Observability helps to take data-driven informed decisions based on runtime Observability telemetry data (logs, metrics, traces, events, ...) and provides insights into SAP & customer services and systems to understand and improve efficiency, scalability, resilience, and availability.

When you seek for fully detailed observability data of your application, you can use SAP Cloud Logging. SAP Cloud Logging allows you to slice and dice logs, metrics, and traces in order to analyze functional and performance behaviour of your BTP workload on Cloud Foundry and Kyma.

Metrics are "measurements captured at runtime", which help you understand your app's health and performance.

Out of the box, @cap-js/telemetry offers metrics regarding the app's database pool, namely the pool info statistics of generic-pool.

@cap-js/telemetry is a CDS plugin providing observability features, including automatic OpenTelemetry instrumentation.

Documentation can be found at cap.cloud.sap and opentelemetry.io.

Prerequisite:

1. You have Incident Management Application configured till this step

Setup Metrics collection

Metrics are "measurements captured at runtime", which help you understand your app's health and performance.

Out of the box, @cap-js/telemetry offers metrics regarding the app's database pool, namely the pool info statistics of generic-pool.

Run and test locally

Once the application has all the requried configurations. The application can be tested locally.

  1. In the terminal run the application with cds watch

  2. Open the application and create a incident.

  3. Check the logs in terminal

    Example db pool metrics printed to the console:

    [telemetry] - db.pool:
    size | available | pending
      1/1 |       1/1 |       0
     

Adding custom metrics to the application

The business user could check how many "High" incidents are being currently handled in the system using the custom metrics. Custom metrics can be added as shown in the following example:

  1. Create a new file at root of the application, name it server.js and paste the following code:
     const cds = require('@sap/cds')
 // Import necessary modules
 const { metrics } = require('@opentelemetry/api');

 const meter = metrics.getMeter('@capire/incidents:incidents.urgency.high');
 // Create a counter
 const counter = meter.createUpDownCounter('incidents.urgency.high');

 cds.on('served', async () => {
 const { ProcessorService } = cds.services
 // Increase count when new incident with high urgency is created
 ProcessorService.after("CREATE", "Incidents", (results, req) => {
     if (results.urgency_code === "H" && results.status_code !== "C") {
     counter.add(1, { 'sap.tenancy.tenant_id': req.tenant });
     }
 });
 // Reduce count once incident is closed
 ProcessorService.after("UPDATE", "Incidents", (results, req) => {
     if (results.urgency_code === "H" && results?.status_code === "C") {
     counter.add(-1, { 'sap.tenancy.tenant_id': req.tenant });
     }
 });
 });
 module.exports = cds.server

Deploy the application

  • For BTP Cloud Foundry runtime, follow the steps described here
  • For BTP Kyma runtime, follow the steps described here

Access Application Metrics

  1. On the left side of the screen, click the breadcrumb and choose Discover.
  2. On the Discover Screen, logs-cfsyslog-* is selected by default. You can see the application logs.
  3. Choose logs-cfsyslog-*, and dropdown menu appears. Select metrics-otel-v1-*. This will display the metrics from the application.
  4. Select the fields from the left side of the screen to view specific fields. The output should be displayed like this:

Create Visualisation for Custom Metrics

In the previous chapter, you created a custom metric to count the "High" urgency incidents. You can create a custom visualisation to show the count.

  1. On the left side breadcrumb, choose Visualize.

  2. Choose Create visualization.

  3. A dialog appears. Choose Metric.

  4. On the right side of the screen, choose Metric.

  5. Choose the dropdown under Aggregation and select Top Hit.

  6. Choose the Field dropdown menu and select the value.

  7. Under the Custom label, enter a name such as High Urgency Incidents.

  8. Choose Update at the bottom of the screen.

  9. The visualisation will be created. 

     

     

     

    You can save this visualisation for quick access.

 #Observability #opentelemetry #SAPCloudLogging #SAPBTP #BTP #Metrics

entry.title: EWM Performance issue due to growth of table /SCWM/STOCK_IW02
entry.content:
Hello EWM Experts,

In production environment, we noticed abnormal growth of SAP table /SCWM/STOCK_IW02, and found this SAP note below regarding it:

2019324 - General performance issues in EWM

We then tried to apply this note / tool in our quality environment, by scheduling this report /LIME/BACKGROUND_DELETE_EXEC as recommended in the note, to run daily in a rather busy system we have.

In most of the days when the job is running, we noticed related SM13 error as following:

Function Module        /SCWM/GM_UPDATE

Status                 Update was canceled

Report                 /SCWM/LHU_TO_UPDF23

Row                    310

Error details   Class:     /LIME/CORE Number:    134
Object is already locked: /LIME/E_RESERVE; parameters: /LIME/E_RESERVE
 /LIME/E_RESERVE /LIME/E_RESERVE

 

Therefore, we can't use this in our productive environment. 

We would like to know if anybody also faced this increase of /SCWM/STOCK_IW02 table and how do you resolve it? 

Thanks in advance!

Best Regards, 
Shiv

entry.title: Proper URL for access to SAC from an external platform
entry.content:
Dear all.

I am trying to create a proper URL for access to a SAC report from an external platform, i.e. Sales Force. The  SAC report is based on an Bex query. The Bex query has only one mandatory, input-ready variable GCO_TIME_REF (manual, not Customer Exit), which is based on a characteristics of type Date (0DATE).

The URL is as of the following:

https://xxx.eu1.sapanalytics.cloud/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/011/app.html#/story2&/s2/9FC813066083DC87D2B1D7EEBA7B2DBD/?v01Model=Cev4161j0g62i4beei83f1t183v&v01Par=GCO_TIME_REF&v01Val=20240625&mode=view

Due to data security puposes, I have replaced the first part of the URL, which refers to the tenant, with xxx. 

Somehow the URL doesn't work. When ever i tried to open it, i just landed on the popup windows, where i have to insert the value of the variable GCO_TIME_REF manually. The date "20240625" of the URL is somehow not accepted.

Can someone please tell me why?

Many thanks in advance

BR

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan Call Dates called earlier than Call...
entry.content:
Greetings,

as per documentation:

So according to your maintenance plan a call object should be created 45 days before the cycle 1000 hours is reached. The estimated date when cycle is reached is calculated based on counter annual estimate and regular measurement documents input.

You can also try clicking on the call algorithm button in IP10. System should show how it calculated call number data.

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Issues with Job Requisition Creation and Recruiter Assignment in Position EC
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We have a customer requirement where the hiring manager creates a job requisition from Position EC. Our process involves:

  1. Hiring managers create job requisitions from Position EC without an assigned recruiter.
  2. Customer requests to route the requisition to a recruiting team group for an available recruiter to self-assign to assist the manager in completing the job requisition and proceeding with the approvals. 
  3. The route map designed for job requisition was initiated as a collaborative step between the hiring manager and the recruiter and then routed to the HR manager.

I've set the recruiter as a collaborative step in the draft and modify stage of the route map. However, the recruiter field is empty at creation, causing permission issues. Also, recruiting team users can view unapproved jobs but can't edit them until approval, so the job requisition will be stuck at the first stage.

Any advice or thoughts would be highly appreciated!
